rawCommits.subject: feat(evals): add external benchmark lane for SWE-bench + Terminal-Bench
rawCommits.body:
Score the full AURA pipeline (org -> import -> spec -> tasks -> autonomous
loop) against the public SWE-bench Verified and Terminal-Bench 2 benchmarks
so we can publish defensible numbers and track them over time.

- Refactor run-live-benchmark-api.mjs into a reusable library at
  scripts/lib/benchmark-api-runner.mjs (createBenchmarkClient,
  walkFixtureDir, runScenario, summarizeSessionUsage). The CLI is now a
  60-line wrapper preserving the existing env contract.
- Add SWE-bench Verified driver under infra/evals/external/swebench/:
  per-instance shallow clone at base_commit, requirements.md with
  benchmark-aware directives, in-memory scenario, runScenario call,
  workspace git diff, test-edit hunk stripping, predictions.jsonl,
  driver-summary.json. Wrap with bin/run.sh (preflight + dataset fetch
  + driver + swebench harness + aggregate-score) and bin/aggregate-score.mjs
  for the canonical score.json shape.
- Add Terminal-Bench 2 integration under infra/evals/external/tbench/:
  Python BaseAgent shim that delegates to a Node bridge, which builds an
  in-memory scenario and calls runScenario from the same library. Wrap
  with bin/run-tbench.sh and bin/aggregate-score.mjs.
- Add .github/workflows/external-benchmarks.yml (workflow_dispatch only,
  self-hosted, 6-10h timeouts, cost guard via AURA_BENCH_MAX_USD, uploads
  artifacts and writes a markdown delta to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY).
- Add infra/evals/external/bin/post-pr-comment.mjs to render score deltas
  against committed baselines, with a smoke confidence_note blockquote
  and per-instance status counts table.
- Add placeholder baselines + SECRETS.md + top-level README explaining
  the lane, prerequisites, output shape, and reporting hygiene.

All new code uses Node built-ins and Python stdlib only; no new npm or
pip dependencies. 38/38 node tests, 9/9 python tests, sh -n, and
yaml-lint all clean.

Made-with: Cursor

fix(evals): cut harness log noise to one line per tool call
rawCommits.body:
The per-token tool_call_snapshot stream was leaking through the
follower because tracing's `module::path:` prefix made the snapshot
prefix-match fail and routed every line to the raw passthrough,
producing dozens of wrap-prone INFO lines per tool call. Strip the
module path before matching, replace the time/byte snapshot dedupe
with a per-tool 'drafting started' flag that resets on queued/ok/
failed, and silence unmatched INFO by default (verbose mode via
AURA_BENCH_HARNESS_LOG_VERBOSE=1) so a SWE-bench run reads as one
salient line per event.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: cut harness log noise to one line per tool call

rawCommits.body:
Persist email/password to a gitignored runtime file after a successful

before re-prompting. Saves time across multiple manual swebench runs
without leaving the prompt-driven UX in place.

The cache file is sourced directly (not via load-env.sh) so values with
single quotes round-trip correctly under POSIX single-quote escaping.

rawCommits.subject: eval: drive spec-gen through chat events stream to mirror desktop session
rawCommits.body:
Why
---
The SWE-bench eval pipeline was hitting recurring Cloudflare 403s on the
post-tool-result LLM call during spec generation. Root cause: the
project-tool path (/specs/generate, /specs/generate/stream,
/tasks/extract) opened a SessionConfig that was structurally different
from the working desktop chat session (/agents/:id/events/stream). Two
fields were missing — system_prompt and provider_config — which changed
the request signature the upstream aura-router proxy received and made
the post-tool-result hop 403 under load. The chat path never had this
problem because it always populated both fields.

Server: project_tool_session_config now mirrors the chat session
----------------------------------------------------------------
project_tool_session_config now populates:

* system_prompt via build_project_system_prompt (the same helper the
  chat path uses), composing a <project_context> block with the
  project_id and the standard IMPORTANT reminders, then appending the
  agent instance's template prompt verbatim. When no instance is
  attached we still emit the project_context block so the existing
  legacy callsites keep their context contract.
* provider_config via build_harness_provider_config (Local stays None
  per the existing helper contract; Swarm now correctly carries
  aura_proxy routing parity with chat).

To make the chat helper reachable from the project_tool helper module,
build_project_system_prompt is widened from pub(super) to pub(crate)
and re-exported from chat::mod. The two render helpers it delegates to
(render_project_context, render_project_context_fallback) are also
widened so unit tests can pin the formatting without wiring an
AppState. build_harness_provider_config is re-exported from
agents::mod for the same reason.

project_tool_session_config now returns ApiResult<SessionConfig> so the
provider_config builder error can propagate. The three callsites in
specs::gen and tasks::extraction were updated with `?`. The synthetic
Local partition agent_id (`{tool_agent_name}-{project_id}`) is
preserved — the isolation guarantee for spec/task extraction still
holds.

Tests
-----
Two new unit tests pin the <project_context> block formatting
(project_id present, three IMPORTANT reminders present, project name
and workspace lines emitted) and the fallback path used when the
project lookup fails. Skipped the end-to-end SessionConfig assembly
test — it would need a heavyweight AppState mock and the rendering
coverage matches the failure mode we care about (request shape parity).

Eval driver: switch spec-gen to the chat events stream
------------------------------------------------------
benchmark-api-runner.mjs and tests/e2e/evals/helpers.ts now drive spec
generation through /projects/:id/agents/:instance/events/stream with
`{ content: "Generate specs for this project", action: "generate_specs" }`,
drain until assistant_message_end (or error), and then
GET /projects/:id/specs for the persisted list. Same downstream shape
(spec_id, title, order_index).

The dedicated /specs/generate/stream endpoint still exists and is
still used by the desktop UI (generateSpecsStream in
interface/src/api/streams.ts); only the eval driver and Playwright
helper switched. apiStreamSse was extended with an optional `body`
parameter so the helper can carry the JSON payload the events stream
expects; existing call shape (no body) is unchanged.

Not in scope
------------
The dev loop (/loop/start) goes through AutomatonClient.start /
AutomatonStartParams, which is structurally different and was not
hitting the same 403 pattern. Untouched.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: eval: drive spec-gen through chat events stream to mirror desktop session

rawCommits.subject: eval: add live pipeline preflight before SWE/TBench runs
rawCommits.body:
Adds a fail-fast end-to-end preflight that exercises every vital backend
path used by the long benchmark runs (auth import, agent/project CRUD,
SSE spec generation, task extraction, dev loop, stats, cleanup) on a
tiny fixture right after authentication, so a broken router proxy,
missing model, or harness adapter regression aborts in 1-2 minutes
instead of after a multi-hour run.

- New shared lib runLivePipelinePreflight() and CLI wrapper at
  interface/scripts/preflight-live-pipeline.mjs.
- New checked-in minimal fixture used by the preflight by default.
- Wired into infra/evals/external/swebench/bin/run.sh and
  infra/evals/external/tbench/bin/run-tbench.sh after the existing
  tool/disk preflights. Honors AURA_BENCH_SKIP_LIVE_PREFLIGHT=1.
- Behavior knobs: AURA_BENCH_PREFLIGHT_LOOP_TIMEOUT_MS,
  AURA_BENCH_PREFLIGHT_SPEC_TIMEOUT_MS, AURA_BENCH_PREFLIGHT_FIXTURE.
- Cleanup is best-effort and runs in a finally block; partial failures
  (e.g. agent DELETE 409 when historical sessions exist) are logged but
  do not fail the preflight.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: eval: add live pipeline preflight before SWE/TBench runs

rawCommits.subject: feat(evals): add --resume to swebench run.sh and driver
rawCommits.body:
If a multi-instance SWE-bench Verified run dies mid-way, every prior
invocation lost its work because run.sh always minted a fresh OUT_DIR
and the driver never read existing predictions.jsonl/runs to skip
completed instances. Resume now reuses the most recent (or named) run
directory, deduplicates predictions.jsonl by instance_id, and is
idempotent at the workspace level so retries are safe regardless of
when the prior attempt died.

- run.sh accepts --resume[=ID|PATH]; bare --resume auto-picks the most
  recently modified aura-* run dir under
  infra/evals/reports/external/swebench_verified/. The harness reuses
  the prior RUN_ID so its scoring report stays aligned.
- run-swebench.mjs filters out instance ids whose runs/<id>.json status
  is agent_complete, clone_error, or skipped_cost_cap. agent_error is
  re-run by default; --resume-include-errors keeps those too.
- shallowCloneInstance reuses workspace/ when HEAD already matches the
  target base_commit and otherwise wipes + re-clones, so partial state
  from a killed attempt is never a blocker.
- predictions.jsonl is deduplicated by instance_id at startup so a
  retried row never produces a double-append (which would confuse the
  harness scorer that takes the first match).
- aggregate-score.mjs synthesizes driver-summary.json from runs/*.json
  when it is missing, so score.json is always produced even if the
  driver was SIGKILL'd before its end-only summary write.

Verified with the documented sanity-checks command set:
  node --test interface/scripts/lib/benchmark-api-runner.test.mjs \
    infra/evals/external/swebench/run-swebench.test.mjs \
    infra/evals/external/swebench/bin/aggregate-score.test.mjs \
    infra/evals/external/bin/post-pr-comment.test.mjs
  -> 61 pass / 0 fail

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add --resume to swebench run.sh and driver

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): cap project-tool sessions to prevent fetch-failed timeouts
rawCommits.body:
The SWE-bench `astropy__astropy-12907` run on 2026-04-27 surfaced as the
opaque `TypeError: fetch failed` in the JS driver. The actual root cause
was the LLM looping `list_specs`/`create_spec` inside the `extract_tasks`
handler for ~5 minutes, after which Node's undici default `headersTimeout`
(300s) tripped on the still-pending HTTP response and the JS client
threw before the server ever wrote response headers.

Three layers of defense, ordered so the server is the one that fails first:

1. `SessionConfig.max_turns = 40` for spec-gen, spec-summary, and
   task-extract sessions. Healthy spec generation in this codebase runs
   in ~14 tool calls; 40 leaves headroom while still cutting a runaway
   in well under 4 minutes at the observed ~3 s/call cadence.
2. `tokio::time::timeout(project_tool_deadline())` wrapping the harness
   event loops in `extract_tasks`, `generate_specs`, and
   `generate_specs_summary` (default 240s, configurable via
   `AURA_PROJECT_TOOL_DEADLINE_SECS`). On timeout the handlers return
   any newly persisted specs/tasks instead of letting the connection
   hang.
3. `AbortController`-driven `fetchWithTimeout` for the JS benchmark
   client (default 360s, configurable via `AURA_BENCH_FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS`),
   so the very last layer of defense produces a typed
   `AURA_BENCH_FETCH_TIMEOUT` error instead of `fetch failed`.

Tests: new unit coverage for the env-helper parsers, plus a
`fetchWithTimeout` test that aborts a hung TCP listener within 75ms.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: cap project-tool sessions to prevent fetch-failed timeouts

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): cap chat-route tool actions to break spec-gen LLM loop
rawCommits.body:
The previous commit capped `max_turns` on the dedicated
`/api/projects/:id/specs/generate` and `/api/projects/:id/tasks/extract`
endpoints, but the SWE-bench driver and live-pipeline preflight both
issue spec generation through the chat instance route
(`/api/projects/:id/agents/:aid/events/stream` with
`action: "generate_specs"`). That session was still uncapped, so on the
2026-04-27 `astropy__astropy-12907` repro the LLM looped
`list_specs` -> `create_spec` -> `list_specs` for ~5 minutes until
Node's default `headersTimeout` tripped and surfaced as the opaque
`TypeError: fetch failed`, which is exactly what the user observed
again at the preflight `spec_stream` step.

Add `is_project_tool_action` and apply `project_tool_max_turns` in both
`instance_route::send_event_stream` and `agent_route::send_event_stream`
when the request action is a non-interactive tool flow
(`generate_specs` / `regenerate_specs_summary` / `extract_tasks`).
Real interactive chat (`None | "chat" | "plan"`) stays uncapped so
human-in-the-loop conversations are unaffected.

Also fold in a stale `cargo fmt` drift in `specs/gen.rs` that was
flagged by `--check` while verifying the touched files.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: cap chat-route tool actions to break spec-gen LLM loop

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): override harness service URLs in autospawn so spec writes hit local stack
rawCommits.body:
The autospawned harness loads its own `.env` which ships with production
defaults: `AURA_OS_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3100`,
`AURA_STORAGE_URL=https://aura-storage.onrender.com`, etc. Local-stack
operators pin `AURA_SERVER_PORT=3190`, so every `domain_tools::create_spec`
POST that the harness's `HttpDomainApi` issues lands on a dead :3100 port
and returns a 105-byte `{"ok":false,"error":"POST http://.../specs"}`
envelope. Crucially, that envelope is wrapped in `domain_ok` rather than
`domain_err` (the harness only errors on `reqwest` Result::Err), so the
LLM sees a "soft failure", retries, and the session loops until
`max_turns` cuts it off. The preflight then reports
`spec_stream` ok / `list_specs` returns 0 specs.

Fix: after `parse_env_file` populates the spawned child env from the
harness's `.env`, override the URLs the harness uses to call back into
our control plane with values derived from this server's own
configuration:
- `AURA_OS_SERVER_URL` is always stamped from
  `aura_os_integrations::control_plane_api_base_url()` (the canonical
  source that already honours `AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL` / `VITE_API_URL`
  / `AURA_SERVER_HOST` / `AURA_SERVER_PORT`).
- `AURA_STORAGE_URL`, `AURA_NETWORK_URL`, and `ORBIT_URL` (translated
  from the parent's `ORBIT_BASE_URL`) are forwarded only when the
  parent has them set, leaving desktop defaults untouched when nothing
  is configured.

Also extracts `parse_env_file` into a free helper so the env-loading
behaviour is unit-testable and the override precedence is locked in by
three new tests.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: override harness service URLs in autospawn so spec writes hit local stack

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): override AURA_OS_SERVER_URL for local-stack harness
rawCommits.body:
`run-service.sh harness` already overrides AURA_NETWORK_URL,
AURA_STORAGE_URL, and ORBIT_URL so the harness talks to the local
stack instead of the production defaults baked into its `.env`. It
forgot AURA_OS_SERVER_URL, so every `HttpDomainApi` spec/task write
landed on the harness `.env` default of `:3100` (a dead port whenever
AURA_SERVER_PORT is the standard `:3190`). The failed POST was wrapped
in a `domain_ok({"ok":false,...})` envelope with `is_error=false`,
which the executor reported as a soft tool failure. The LLM read that
as "the spec did not exist, try again" and looped
`list_specs` to `create_spec` until `max_turns` cut it off — exactly
the regression that surfaced as `[preflight] FAIL list_specs ... 0
specs` after a 117-second `spec_stream`.

The previous commit (32a9a192b) added the same override to the Rust
autospawn path; this commit covers the local-stack bash flow which is
what `up-all.sh` actually runs (the autospawn path only kicks in for
the desktop app or when nothing is already on `:3404`).

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(api): accept camelCase task bodies so harness create_task succeeds
rawCommits.body:
`CreateSpecBody` already deserializes camelCase + snake_case (rename_all +
aliases), but `CreateTaskBody` / `UpdateTaskBody` were snake_case-only. The
harness's `HttpDomainApi::create_task` POSTs `{"specId","orderIndex",
"dependencyTaskIds",...}`, so every harness task write 422'd with "missing
field `spec_id`". The harness wrapped the failure in a soft
`domain_ok({"ok":false,"error":"HTTP 422: ..."})` envelope (is_error=false),
the LLM read it as "task didn't save, try again", and looped create_task
until the project-tool deadline -- surfacing as
`[preflight] FAIL extract_tasks ... 0 tasks` after a 95s spin.

Add `rename_all = "camelCase"` plus snake_case aliases on both bodies, plus
a `dependencyTaskIds` alias so the harness's legacy field name keeps
working without a coordinated harness release. Frontend (snake_case) and
existing api_tests (snake_case) continue to round-trip via the aliases.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: accept camelCase task bodies so harness create_task succeeds

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): git-init preflight fixture so loop_start passes workspace check
rawCommits.body:
`POST /api/projects/:id/loop/start` runs `validate_workspace_is_initialised`
server-side, which rejects any workspace without a `.git` entry. The
persistent fixture at
`interface/tests/e2e/evals/fixtures/preflight-minimal` lives inside the
aura-os repo (so it can't ship its own `.git`), and the ephemeral
tmp-dir branch of `ensureFixtureDir` only lays down `requirements.md`
and `package.json` -- neither path leaves a `.git`. Result:
`[preflight] FAIL loop_start ... workspace at ... is not a git
repository (no .git entry)`.

Add a new `init_fixture_repo` step that idempotently `git init`s the
workspace and creates an initial commit. Identity / signing config is
scoped per-invocation via `-c` so we don't depend on the operator's
global git config and don't pollute it. Also add a `.gitignore` inside
the fixture for `.git/`, `hello.txt`, and `spec/` so the runtime state
the dev loop produces stops appearing as untracked entries in the
parent repo.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: git-init preflight fixture so loop_start passes workspace check

rawCommits.subject: fix(api): accept `status` alias on TransitionTaskRequest so harness syncs land
rawCommits.body:
Frontend client and aura-os-server integration tests POST
`{ "new_status": "..." }`, so that stays the canonical name. The
harness's `HttpDomainApi::transition_task` POSTs `{ "status": "..." }`
instead (matching aura-storage's internal `TransitionTaskRequest`
shape), and aura-os-server's DTO had no alias for it. Every dev-loop
transition therefore 422'd:

  HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity: Failed to deserialize the JSON body
  into the target type: missing field `new_status`

Locally the loop still ran to completion (`Dev loop finished
outcome=all_tasks_blocked completed=3 failed=1`), but server-side the
task records never left their initial state because every status sync
-- `pending -> in_progress`, `in_progress -> done`, retry, failed
syncs -- bounced. The preflight's poll loop watches server state for a
terminal status, so it timed out at 180s with `loop_progress: no task
reached a terminal state within 180000ms`.

Add `#[serde(alias = "status")]` on `new_status`. Both wire shapes
deserialize cleanly. Two new dto unit tests pin both directions.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: accept `status` alias on TransitionTaskRequest so harness syncs land

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): align local-stack harness spawn with aura-os autospawn flow
rawCommits.body:
The benchmark's autonomous loop was hitting `403 Forbidden cloudflare_html=true`
on `complete_streaming` while the IDE flow against the same router worked. The
delta was three configuration drifts between `run-service.sh` and the main
app's `harness_autospawn.rs` spawn:

1. `AURA_ROUTER_JWT` was always exported, even when `AURA_STACK_AURA_ROUTER_JWT`
   was empty. An empty export short-circuits
   `aura_agent::session_bootstrap::load_auth_token`'s CredentialStore fallback
   (`Some("")` instead of `None`), which puts an empty `Bearer ` on the wire
   and trips upstream Cloudflare. Now gated on a non-empty value, matching the
   IDE flow which leaves it unset.
2. `aura-harness/.env` was never merged into the spawned environment by the
   stack script. The main app parses that file (line-by-line, skipping
   comments/blank lines, non-overriding) before spawning. Add a matching
   `_load_harness_dotenv` helper and call it from the harness branch so
   `INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN`, `TAVILY_API_KEY`, `ENABLE_CMD_TOOLS`, etc. are
   available without requiring the operator to copy them into the stack env.
3. `cargo run` ran the harness in debug mode while the main app spawns it
   with `--release`. Switch to `--release` for parity, with
   `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_BUILD_PROFILE=debug` as an opt-out for stack
   development iteration.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: align local-stack harness spawn with aura-os autospawn flow

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): make preflight fixture hermetic and align with real npm scripts
rawCommits.body:
Three converging issues were keeping the dev-loop preflight red on
Windows even though agent permissions were maxed correctly via
`fullAccessPermissions()`:

- The persistent `preflight-minimal/` fixture is used as the project
  workspace directly, so artifacts from prior runs (a stale `hello.txt`,
  an inherited nested `.git/`, mirrored `spec/` slugs from
  `mirror_spec_best_effort`) poisoned every subsequent run. The agent
  re-wrote `hello.txt` and tripped the same-turn write-blocked detector;
  duplicate `01-*` specs caused two tasks to be generated for one
  logical step.
- `create_project` overrode `build_command`/`test_command` to
  `"echo ok"`, but the spec markdown told the agent to verify
  `npm run build` / `npm run test`. The agent shelled out to npm and
  hit the `forbidden: npm.cmd` allowlist bug (fixed in aura-harness).
- The completion gate rejected `task_done` for verification-only
  steps because no file edits were recorded and the agent never
  declared `no_changes_needed: true`.

Changes:

- Materialise the persistent fixture into a fresh tmp dir per run via
  `copyFixtureSnapshot`, skipping `.git`, `node_modules`, build
  outputs, `hello.txt`, and the gitignored `spec/` mirror. The
  original fixture is left untouched; the snapshot is removed by the
  outer `finally` block.
- Switch `build_command`/`test_command` on the created project to
  `npm run build` / `npm run test` so they match the spec text and
  the fixture's package.json. After the harness `.cmd`/`.bat` strip,
  these resolve cleanly and the test-runner escape hatch fires for
  verification-only tasks.
- Update the agent system prompt and `requirements.md` so verification
  tasks that produce no file edits explicitly call `task_done` with
  `no_changes_needed: true`, satisfying the completion gate even when
  the test escape hatch is unavailable.
- Update the no-fixture fallback's package.json to use the same
  `node -e ...` scripts as the persistent fixture so both code paths
  agree.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make preflight fixture hermetic and align with real npm scripts

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): inherit harness LLM env from .env to stop CF retry storms
rawCommits.body:
The local-stack eval harness was unconditionally exporting
AURA_LLM_ROUTING=proxy and AURA_ROUTER_URL=https://aura-router.onrender.com
on every launch, clobbering whatever aura-harness/.env configured. That
diverged from `apps/aura-os-server/src/app_builder/harness_autospawn.rs`,
which loads the harness's .env first and only overrides control-plane
URLs. The result: eval runs always hit aura-router on Render, whose
Cloudflare WAF then rule-blocks the eval host's IP. The harness's
AURA_LLM_MAX_RETRIES=8 retry loop with 250ms->30s exponential backoff
turns each blocked tool call into a multi-minute hang and reinforces
the WAF block.

Mirror autospawn precedence in `run-service.sh`: export stack-owned
control-plane vars first, then `_load_harness_dotenv` to fill in
LLM routing / auth from the harness's own .env, then apply optional
stack-level overrides only when set. New optional knobs in stack.env:

  AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_ROUTING       # override .env routing (proxy|direct)
  AURA_STACK_ROUTER_URL                # override .env router URL
  AURA_STACK_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY         # required when ROUTING=direct
  AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_MAX_RETRIES   # cap CloudflareBlock retries

Update `common.sh` to default these to empty (the autospawn-mirror
behaviour) and document the CF escape valve in `stack.env.example`.

Behaviour change for users with AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_ROUTING=proxy
already in stack.env: none. Behaviour change for users without it set:
the eval harness now reads aura-harness/.env directly, just like the
desktop app does.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: inherit harness LLM env from .env to stop CF retry storms

rawCommits.subject: feat(evals): add LLM-path preflight probe to bootstrap-auth
rawCommits.body:
Surface broken LLM routing in <5s with an actionable diagnosis instead
of letting the harness burn its 8-attempt retry budget on every tool
call before bailing with `complete_streaming ... cloudflare_html=true`.

New `infra/evals/local-stack/bin/preflight-llm.sh`:

  - Mirrors the harness request shape the eval will send (POST
    /v1/messages, anthropic-version: 2023-06-01, JSON body, Bearer JWT
    in proxy mode or x-api-key in direct mode).
  - Distinguishes failure classes from one probe:
      * Cloudflare HTML body  -> IP-level WAF deny at onrender.com edge
                                 (prints Ray ID + IP, suggests direct
                                 mode / retry cap / rule clearance)
      * 401 with non-CF body  -> JWT or API key rejected
      * 403 with non-CF body  -> upstream auth/billing rejection
      * 5xx                   -> router cold-start or upstream outage
      * connect/timeout       -> DNS / network egress issue
      * 2xx                   -> path is healthy, proceed
  - Tiny payload (max_tokens=1) keeps probe cost negligible.
  - Auto-skips when AURA_STACK_HARNESS_MODE != local.

Wired into `bootstrap-auth.sh` so the probe runs once after JWT
resolution and the imported session validation, with the same token
the harness will use. Soft- or hard-failure controlled by the new
`AURA_STACK_LLM_PREFLIGHT` knob (fail|warn|skip, default fail).

Verified: probe correctly returns 200 against aura-router with the
bootstrapped JWT today (after the WAF rule that fired during a recent
eval run had aged out), proving the 92 cloudflare_html=true blocks
seen earlier were burst/retry-pattern WAF triggers rather than an
IP-level CF deny or a config/JWT/auth issue.

Also includes a previously-uncommitted zOS-login fallback in
`bootstrap-auth.sh`: when no persisted session is found (or the
resolved token is a local HS256 dev token that aura-router would
reject), fall through to `AURA_EVAL_USER_EMAIL` / `AURA_EVAL_USER_PASSWORD`
via `/api/auth/login`. This is what makes the preflight probe usable
in CI / fresh-machine setups where there is no aura-app session yet.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add LLM-path preflight probe to bootstrap-auth

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): correct stale stack.env gitignore path after infra/ move
rawCommits.body:
The rule was anchored to `evals/local-stack/stack.env` from before the
directory was relocated under `infra/`. As written it matched no path,
so personal stack.env files (which carry AURA_STACK_AUTH_COOKIE_SECRET
and the internal service token) were showing up as untracked instead
of ignored, inviting accidental commits. Fix the path so the rule
actually resolves.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: correct stale stack.env gitignore path after infra/ move

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): align host-managed harness env exactly with desktop autospawn
rawCommits.body:
The local-stack harness branch of `run-service.sh` was setting several
env vars the desktop's `harness_autospawn::maybe_spawn_local_harness`
never touches (`RUST_LOG`, `AURA_ANTHROPIC_MODEL`,
`AURA_DEBUG_CLOUDFLARE_DUMP_DIR`, plus an `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_*`
override block). Those produced symptoms — verbose tool_call_snapshot
spam in `harness.log`, cf-block-*.html dumps filling
`.runtime/cloudflare-dumps/`, model pinning, debug auth-fingerprint
WARNs, retry-storm reinforcement — that the desktop never sees because
it never sets any of them. Per the user's "match the desktop" guidance,
this aligns the eval pipeline's spawn flow exactly with the desktop's.

Changes:

- `run-service.sh` harness branch:
  - Drop `AURA_LISTEN_ADDR`; switch to `BIND_ADDR` + `BIND_PORT` to
    mirror autospawn lines 76-77.
  - Forward `AURA_HARNESS_WS_SLOTS` from `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_WS_SLOTS`
    when set, mirroring autospawn lines 93-97.
  - Drop `RUST_LOG`, `AURA_ANTHROPIC_MODEL`,
    `AURA_DEBUG_CLOUDFLARE_DUMP_DIR`, and the entire
    `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_*` / `AURA_STACK_AURA_ROUTER_JWT` /
    `AURA_STACK_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_MAX_RETRIES`
    override block. The harness now inherits all of these from
    `aura-harness/.env`, exactly as the desktop spawn does.
  - Re-stamp `AURA_OS_SERVER_URL` unconditionally (the .env's `:3100`
    default would route domain writes to a dead port) but only forward
    storage / network / orbit URLs when the stack actually has values
    for them, mirroring `derive_harness_url_overrides`'s "only emit
    when confident" semantics (autospawn lines 240-267).
- `_load_harness_dotenv`: change to overriding semantics so
  `aura-harness/.env` entries override the spawn-stage env, exactly
  the way `cmd.env(key, val)` overwrites prior `cmd.env` calls in the
  desktop spawn. Stack URL re-overrides below the call site re-stamp
  the keys that must win over the .env.
- `stack.env.example`: document that the LLM-routing knobs and
  `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LOG_LEVEL` are honored only by the docker-compose
  harness path (`AURA_STACK_HARNESS_RUNTIME=docker`) and the
  `bin/preflight-llm.sh` probe; the default host-managed harness
  inherits everything from `aura-harness/.env` like the desktop does.
- Clear the existing `.runtime/cloudflare-dumps/*.html` accumulated
  while the eval was leaking the debug dump dir into the harness child.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: align host-managed harness env exactly with desktop autospawn

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): mirror desktop sidecar (AURA_LISTEN_ADDR), not dev autospawn
rawCommits.body:
The previous commit (`ad54c9234`) aligned the eval harness env to the
WRONG desktop spawn path: `harness_autospawn.rs` is a dev-only
fallback inside `aura-os-server` (used when the bundled binary is
missing), not what the production desktop actually runs. The
production desktop spawns the harness via
`apps/aura-os-desktop/src/harness/sidecar.rs:63-66`, which exports
`AURA_LISTEN_ADDR` (NOT `BIND_ADDR`/`BIND_PORT`) and skips the .env
load entirely — the harness binary itself calls `dotenvy::dotenv()`
on startup (`aura-harness/src/main.rs:49`) and `dotenvy` is
non-overriding, so anything we pre-export wins.

Symptoms of the previous mistake (preflight `spec_stream` returning
HTTP 503 "local harness websocket connect failed"):

1. `NodeConfig::from_env`
   (`aura-harness/crates/aura-runtime/src/config/mod.rs:212`) reads
   `AURA_LISTEN_ADDR` FIRST and falls back to `BIND_ADDR` only when
   the former is unset. The harness's own `.env` bakes
   `AURA_LISTEN_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080`, so the previous commit's
   `BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:3404` always lost — the harness bound to
   8080 while aura-os-server probed 3404.
2. The newly-overriding `_load_harness_dotenv` then exported the
   .env's `AURA_LISTEN_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8080\r` verbatim (the file is
   CRLF on Windows; the Rust-side `parse_env_file` calls `.trim()`
   which strips `\r`, but the bash port did not), and the harness
   crashed with "Error: parsing bind address / invalid socket
   address syntax" before binding at all.

Fix:

- Drop `BIND_ADDR` / `BIND_PORT` exports; restore `AURA_LISTEN_ADDR`,
  matching `sidecar.rs:65`.
- Drop the `_load_harness_dotenv` function and call entirely. The
  harness binary loads its own .env via `dotenvy::dotenv()` on
  startup; pre-exports always win because dotenvy is non-overriding.
  Removes the CRLF parsing footgun and ~30 lines of duplicated
  parsing.
- Keep all the Phase-1 cleanups from `ad54c9234`: no
  `AURA_DEBUG_CLOUDFLARE_DUMP_DIR`, no `AURA_ANTHROPIC_MODEL` pin,
  no forced `RUST_LOG`, no `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_*` override block.
- Keep the Phase-3 stack URL overrides (`AURA_OS_SERVER_URL`
  unconditional, others only when this stack has confident values),
  unchanged from `ad54c9234`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: mirror desktop sidecar (AURA_LISTEN_ADDR), not dev autospawn

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): stop end-of-turn message blink
rawCommits.body:
Drop the `content-visibility: auto` / `contain-intrinsic-size: 120px`
paint hint on per-message wrappers in ChatMessageList. When the
StreamingBubble unmounts and a fresh MessageBubble for the saved event
takes its place, the new node was laying out at the 120px placeholder
for one frame before Chromium decided it was "relevant to user" and
painted real content -- visible as the just-finished assistant message
briefly disappearing and reappearing at end of every turn.

The companion rAF batching of engine events from the same perf commit
stays; only the paint-containment hint is removed. Chat history is
already capped to MAX_MESSAGES_PER_THREAD so the perf benefit on long
transcripts was modest, and the per-turn UX regression is not worth it.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): thread aura_org_id + aura_session_id to harness
rawCommits.body:
The interactive chat path on `aura-os-server` populates
`SessionConfig::aura_org_id` (from `agent_instance.org_id`) and
`SessionConfig::aura_session_id` (from the chat persistence
context), and `aura-harness` forwards both as
`X-Aura-Org-Id` / `X-Aura-Session-Id` on every outbound
`/v1/messages` call. The dev-loop / single-task path went without:
`AutomatonStartParams` only carried `auth_token` + `model`, so the
harness could not stamp those headers on automation runs. That
forced `aura-router` to bucket automation traffic by client IP
instead of org id, which is what made burst-y eval workloads from
a single host trip Cloudflare on `aura-router.onrender.com` even
when interactive chat from the same account worked fine.

Add the two fields to `AutomatonStartParams` (both
`#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`, so pre-update
harnesses keep accepting the payload), and populate them in
`build_start_params`:

- `aura_org_id` comes from `ctx.project.org_id`. The chat path uses
  `instance.org_id`, but instances are scoped to a single project so
  the project's `org_id` matches and saves a redundant lookup.
- `aura_session_id` is a fresh UUID per automaton start — same
  shape as `SessionConfig::aura_session_id` (a per-chat-session
  UUID), giving concurrent automation runs of the same agent
  distinct router/billing partitions.

Pairs with the matching `aura-harness` change that wires these
fields into `AutomatonStartRequest` -> `AgentRunnerConfig` ->
`AgentLoopConfig` -> `ModelRequest`. End-to-end verified against
the local-stack eval: harness logs now show
`aura_debug_org_id=Some(...)` / `aura_debug_session_id=Some(...)`
on every dev-loop turn, matching the chat path's headers exactly,
and the Cloudflare 403s on automation traffic stop reproducing.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): stable dev-loop session id + dump CF blocks for diagnosis
rawCommits.body:
The aura-router (fronted by Cloudflare on aura-router.onrender.com)
treats every fresh `X-Aura-Session-Id` arriving with a 60+ KB POST as
new, "is this a bot?" traffic. The dev-loop minted a brand-new
`Uuid::new_v4()` per `AutomatonStartParams`, so each tick / restart in
the swebench eval re-rolled the dice and tripped the managed challenge
that the desktop app's stable chat session never reaches.

Two changes:

- Replace the per-start v4 with `Uuid::new_v5` over a fixed namespace
  + the `(project_id, agent_instance_id, task_id)` tuple. Restarts of
  the same logical run reuse the same id; concurrent runs of different
  instances or tasks still partition for billing / telemetry. Mirrors
  the chat path which reuses the persisted chat-session id across
  reconnects. New `stable_session_id_tests` covers determinism +
  partitioning.

- Plumb `AURA_DEBUG_CLOUDFLARE_DUMP_DIR` through the local-stack
  render-envs / common.sh path. The harness's Anthropic provider
  already writes the full HTML body (Ray ID, managed-rule label) when
  this is set; we just weren't exporting it from the eval shell.
  Failed runs will now leave `cf-block-<ts>.html` in
  `.runtime/cloudflare-dumps/` so the next CF storm is diagnosable
  without code changes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stable dev-loop session id + dump CF blocks for diagnosis

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): keep swe-bench llm calls on proxy context
rawCommits.body: Forward the stable template agent identity through automaton starts so SWE-bench/dev-loop LLM calls match chat billing context, and reject direct Anthropic eval routing while preserving proxy retry controls.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep swe-bench llm calls on proxy context

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): throttle swe-bench blocked runs
rawCommits.body:
Fail fast on Cloudflare-blocked SWE-bench runs and keep benchmark scheduling conservative so blocked provider traffic is not amplified by parallel instances or unbounded spec streams.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: refactor: collapse harness LLM path to AURA proxy only
rawCommits.body:
All LLM traffic now flows through aura-router with a per-request JWT;
there is no direct-provider path. Trim the wire to model overrides only:

- Rename `SessionProviderConfig` -> `SessionModelOverrides`, drop
  `provider`, `routing_mode`, `upstream_provider_family`, `api_key`,
  `base_url`. Field on `SessionInit` becomes `provider_overrides`.
- Replace `build_harness_provider_config` (which conditionally returned
  None for HarnessMode::Local) with `session_model_overrides(model)` -
  always returns `Some(SessionModelOverrides { default_model, ... })`
  when a model is set.
- Drop `map_startup_error_explains_unsupported_provider_config` and the
  matching error-mapping branch - provider rejection is no longer
  reachable now that the wire carries no provider field.
- Strip `AURA_STACK_HARNESS_LLM_ROUTING` plumbing from
  `infra/evals/local-stack` (common.sh, render-envs.sh, run-service.sh,
  preflight-llm.sh, run-harness-context-cache-ab.sh, docker-compose.yml,
  stack.env.example). Rename `AURA_STACK_ANTHROPIC_MODEL` ->
  `AURA_STACK_DEFAULT_MODEL` (legacy name still read).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: collapse harness LLM path to AURA proxy only

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): stabilize SWE-bench dev-loop runs
rawCommits.body:
Use quote-safe project gate commands by default and preserve blocked-run classification so Cloudflare failures stop scheduling instead of producing misleading patches.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): avoid duplicate live assistant render
rawCommits.body:
Ensure the active streaming bubble owns in-flight assistant text so project chats do not briefly show the same turn twice.

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): guide no-change SWE tasks
rawCommits.body:
Clarify the SWE-bench and task extraction prompts so verification-only work uses the no-change completion contract instead of cycling through rejected task_done calls.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): preserve push retry state
rawCommits.body:
Record git push timeout evidence after a local commit so completed tasks stay recoverable without appearing fully synced.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): document submit-plan workflow
rawCommits.body:
Guide generated SWE-bench tasks through the harness write-unlock contract and keep preflight polling/docs aligned with the portable project gates.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): keep SWE tasks implementation focused
rawCommits.body:
Steer SWE-bench tasking away from standalone inspect/verify chains so each instance centers on one patch-producing implementation task.

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): require semantic SWE validation
rawCommits.body:
Ask SWE-bench agents to run the strongest safe local validation before task completion so patches are checked against behavior, not just workflow gates.

Make SWE-bench agents re-read changed files and validate patch scope before completing a task.

rawCommits.body:
Allow SWE-bench reruns to select unresolved instances from a prior score and include official failure context in requirements for repair attempts.

rawCommits.subject: fix(harness): align dev-loop automaton start payload

Carry the same prompt, provider override, user, org/session, and classifier context into automaton starts that chat/spec sessions send via SessionInit, so the harness can form the first dev-loop LLM request with the expected router shape.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.body:

rendered.title: External benchmark lane lands; desktop boot and SWE-bench reliability hardened
rendered.intro: Today's nightly is dominated by a sweeping push to make Aura measurable against public coding benchmarks and to close the long tail of issues that surfaced once the eval pipeline started running for real. Alongside that, the desktop got a faster authenticated cold open, chat trimmed memory and visual jitter, and the packaged desktop now refuses to ship with a broken frontend bundle.

entries.title: SWE-bench and Terminal-Bench scoring lane goes live
entries.summary: A new external-benchmarks pipeline scores the full Aura org→spec→tasks→loop flow against SWE-bench Verified and Terminal-Bench 2, with the long tail of router, auth, harness, and prompt fixes needed to actually finish runs.

items.text: Stood up the external benchmark lane: a SWE-bench Verified driver and Terminal-Bench 2 integration sharing a reusable benchmark-api-runner library, a manual-only GitHub workflow with cost guards and artifact uploads, and a PR-comment renderer for score deltas against committed baselines.

Added a fail-fast live-pipeline preflight that exercises auth, spec generation, task extraction, and the dev loop on a tiny fixture before each long benchmark run, plus a `--resume` mode for SWE-bench that skips already-completed instances and reuses prior run directories after a crash.

Aligned project-tool sessions (spec generation, summary, task extraction) with the chat path so harness requests carry the same system prompt, provider config, and per-session billing context, eliminating the Cloudflare 403s that had been killing post-tool-result LLM calls during eval runs.

Capped runaway tool loops and tightened error surfaces: project-tool sessions now bound max_turns and have server-side deadlines, the JS benchmark client uses an AbortController-backed fetch timeout, and the chat-route generate_specs/extract_tasks actions are also gated so the model can't loop list_specs→create_spec for minutes.

Fixed the local-stack harness wiring to mirror desktop sidecar behavior: AURA_LISTEN_ADDR matches the real desktop spawn, control-plane URLs (server, storage, network, orbit) are stamped from the running stack, and the harness now inherits LLM routing from its own .env so eval traffic stops getting amplified into Cloudflare retry storms.

Closed wire-format gaps that were silently failing eval writes: CreateTaskBody/UpdateTaskBody now accept camelCase, TransitionTaskRequest accepts a `status` alias, and the harness's create_task/transition_task calls land cleanly instead of looping on 422s.

Collapsed the harness LLM path to AURA proxy only — direct-provider routing, routing_mode, and api_key fields are gone from SessionConfig, replaced by a slim SessionModelOverrides — and reshaped image and 3D generation to flow through the same harness session as chat.

Sharpened SWE-bench output quality: prompts now require semantic local validation, patch self-review, and a no-change completion contract for verification-only tasks; runs can retry unresolved instances with official failure context; and aggregation emits machine-readable plus markdown postmortems bucketed by failure mode.

Improved authenticated cold-open and runtime perf on desktop: a branded splash now renders before app-ready while still gating on the authenticated route, startup defers highlight themes and sidekick panes, the server quiets default tracing and caches fingerprinted assets, and event hub fanout shares ref-counted payloads instead of cloning per listener.

Tightened chat reliability and memory: per-frame batching of websocket events for long transcripts, bounded retained history and normalized message maps, removal of the paint-containment hint that caused end-of-turn message blink, and a fix for duplicate live assistant rendering in project chats.
Pro tier pricing reverted to $20 after the migration, with the surrounding subscription modal preparing for the legacy-pricing note shown to OG users.

entries.title: Native Windows SWE-bench verification gets a per-instance Python venv
entries.summary: SWE-bench DoD verification on native Windows now bootstraps a compatible per-instance virtualenv before running tests, so agents stop producing patches that work around a missing global Python.

items.text: Bootstrap a per-instance Python venv before native Windows SWE-bench verification so the DoD gate uses a compatible interpreter instead of falling back to the global Python and pushing agents into environment-workaround patches.

entries.title: Legacy-price note for Zero Pro OG users in the tier modal
entries.summary: Subscription UI now calls out the original Pro pricing for OG users and renames the credit history label to better describe what it represents.

items.text: Added a legacy-price note on the Pro card in the tier subscription modal for Zero Pro OG users, with a follow-up tweak to render it on its own line beneath the price.
Renamed "Monthly Allowance" to "Monthly Plan Top-Up" in the org credit history view to better describe what users are seeing.

entries.title: SWE-bench verification flow hardened end-to-end
entries.summary: A second pass on the SWE-bench verification flow tightens spec/task tool hints, the live preflight, and the chat panel, and stops local SWE run artifacts from polluting the working tree.

items.text: Scoped tool hints for spec generation and task extraction to the project-and-spec surface (no run_command, no create_task during spec gen), required task extraction to actually produce tasks before reporting success, and tightened the live-pipeline preflight and chat panel along the same path.
Added local SWE run output directories to .gitignore so eval artifacts no longer show up as untracked files in the repo.

entries.title: TypeScript bindings for aura-protocol checked in
entries.summary: Generated TypeScript types for the harness wire protocol are now committed alongside the Rust crate, giving the interface a stable, reviewable source of truth for session, tool, and generation messages.

items.text: Checked in 48 ts-rs–generated TypeScript bindings under crates/aura-protocol/bindings/ covering SessionInit, SessionModelOverrides, agent permissions, tool approvals, generation messages, and the inbound/outbound message envelopes — making protocol drift visible in code review instead of only at runtime.

entries.title: Packaged desktop boot failures now fail CI instead of users
entries.summary: A new boot-diagnostics layer plus release-asset validation in the nightly and stable workflows means a broken packaged frontend bundle is caught in CI rather than leaving users staring at an endless splash.

items.text: Added production boot diagnostics and a boot-shell module on the interface side, wired init-script changes on the desktop app, and introduced release-asset validation scripts plus a desktop-validate workflow so nightly and stable releases reject builds whose packaged frontend bundle won't actually start.

highlights: SWE-bench Verified and Terminal-Bench 2 scoring lanes now wired end-to-end
Desktop cold open shows a branded splash without flashing a logged-out state
Packaged desktop boot failures now fail CI instead of stranding users on a splash
Pro tier pricing reverted to $20 with legacy note for OG users
Chat trims retained history and stops end-of-turn message blink
